skill.name: rn-zustand-patterns
skill.description: Zustand state management patterns for React Native. Use when working with Zustand stores, debugging state timing issues, or implementing async actions in Zustand.
skill.summary: Guides reliable Zustand state patterns for React Native async actions, selectors, tests, and debugging.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer findings for external command execution, weak cryptography, certificate keys, and network access are false positives from Markdown inline code and TypeScript examples. No prompt injection, obfuscation, credential collection, executable scripts, or real outbound network behavior was found. Publication risk is low because examples include debug logging and an analytics comment that users should not copy into production without privacy review.

low_findings.title: Debug Logging May Expose Application State
low_findings.description: The debugging example logs answer values and store state. This is acceptable as educational sample code, but copying it into production could expose sensitive user data in device logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is clearly a documentation example, not executable skill behavior. The logged values are application state, so the risk depends on how users copy the sample.

low_findings.title: Analytics Side Effect Needs Privacy Review
low_findings.description: The subscription example suggests persisting data or sending analytics when answers change. This is not active network code, but users should review consent, minimization, and redaction before implementing it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is only a comment in educational sample code. It still points to a possible data flow that could become sensitive in a real app.

content.user_title: Build Reliable React Native Zustand Stores
content.value_statement: React Native apps can develop subtle Zustand bugs when async actions, selectors, and renders interact. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for stores, tests, and debugging.
actual_capabilities: Explains why Zustand set updates synchronously while React renders are batched.
Guides use of get and getState to avoid stale closures after async work.
Shows patterns for awaitable async actions with loading and error state.
Compares selector approaches for Zustand 4.x and 5.x shallow selection.
Documents testing, reset helpers, subscriptions, and debugging middleware.
limitations: Does not provide a complete generated store for a specific application domain.
Does not replace React Native performance profiling or device testing.
Does not cover every Zustand middleware or persistence strategy.
Example logging and analytics patterns require production privacy review.

use_cases.title: Fix Async Store Bugs
use_cases.description: Diagnose stale values after await calls and restructure actions so callers can wait for state changes.

use_cases.title: Review Store Selectors
use_cases.description: Find selectors that create unnecessary renders and apply shallow selection or multiple selectors.

use_cases.title: Create Store Tests
use_cases.description: Add reset helpers and async flow checks for Zustand stores used in mobile features.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Store Timing
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain why my Zustand store state updates before my React Native component re-renders. Use the rn-zustand-patterns skill.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Stale Closure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Zustand async action for stale closure bugs after await calls. Suggest a safer version using get or getState.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Selectors
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these React Native Zustand selectors for unnecessary re-renders. Recommend Zustand 4.x and 5.x compatible fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tested Store Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Zustand store pattern for this async React Native workflow, including loading state, errors, reset support, and tests.

output_examples.input: My component reads an old value after set runs in a store action.
output_examples.output: The response explains that Zustand state changes immediately, while React renders later. It recommends reading immediate values from getState and updating UI from selectors.

output_examples.input: My async save action overwrites newer answers after a network call.
output_examples.output: The response identifies stale closure risk after await. It suggests re-reading current state before writing and making the action awaitable.

output_examples.input: A selector returns an object and causes many React Native re-renders.
output_examples.output: The response recommends multiple primitive selectors or shallow selection. It distinguishes the import pattern for Zustand 4.x and 5.x.

best_practices: Read current state with get or getState after each await in store actions.
Keep source state in the store and compute derived values in selectors.
Use reset helpers so async store flows can be tested repeatably.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a component has re-rendered immediately after a store set call.
Do not return new selector objects on every render without shallow comparison.
Do not leave verbose state logging in production mobile builds.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill with TypeScript examples for React Native Zustand stores.

faq.question: Can it help with Zustand 5.x?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes migration notes for shallow selectors and improved type inference.

faq.question: Does it support persisted stores?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. It mentions persistence as a side effect, but it does not document a full persistence setup.

faq.question: Can it debug race conditions?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on stale closures, awaitable actions, and re-reading state after async operations.

faq.question: Is it specific to React Native?
faq.answer: The patterns target React Native apps, but many Zustand timing rules also apply to React web apps.

faq.question: Should I copy the logging examples to production?
faq.answer: No. Use them for local debugging and remove or redact logs before release builds.
